texto: Away from the sea I languish, I suffocate, I die! Ah! I believe that the waves roll their foam in my veins, that in my hair the tempest blows. My raptures and my cries are the echo of the ocean, my beauty is but a reflection of its waves. I feel it beat, roar, and abate by turns. Away from the sea all is ennui and sadness.
explicacao: Citação de Deirdre Cavanagh sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Away from the sea I languish, I suffocate, I die! Ah! I believe that the waves roll their foam in…” articula reflexao, ocean, sea em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como sea.
